plainEnglish: At Level AAA, motion animations triggered by user interaction—parallax, large transitions, movement on scroll—can be turned off, unless the animation is essential. Honor the prefers-reduced-motion setting so people with vestibular disorders aren't made dizzy.
whyItMatters: Users with vestibular disorders can feel dizziness or nausea from large motion animations (parallax, scroll effects) that they can't disable.
quickCheck: Enable 'reduce motion' in your OS and confirm non-essential animations are reduced or removed; check that your CSS honors @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce).
commonMistakes: Parallax scrolling or large transition animations with no prefers-reduced-motion fallback.
Ignoring the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) media query entirely.
Motion triggered on scroll or hover that can't be turned off.

codeExamples.label: Animation disabled when reduced motion is preferred
Large parallax motion with no opt-out
